data.title: XRP Price Shatters the War Ceiling as Peace Talks Ignite a Move to $1.60
data.description:
The crypto market is finally breathing a sigh of relief. On March 5, XRP (XRP-USD) climbed to $1.44. This rally took off after reports surfaced that Iran is reaching out to the United States to end the current conflict. With the threat of war cooling down, investors are moving their money out of waiting mode and back into energetic assets like XRP.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Diplomacy Gives Crypto Buyers More Confidence

The mood changed when the New York Times (NYT) reported that Iran sent a message to the CIA through middlemen. They are reportedly looking for a way to stop the fighting. While President Trump said a final deal might take a while, the simple hint of a talk was enough to bring the buyers back in a massive way.

The data shows that people are trading XRP with renewed energy. Trading volume shot up by nearly 40%, hitting $4.1 billion in just 24 hours. It wasn't a lonely move, either; the total crypto market grew to $2.49 trillion, with Bitcoin (BTC-USD) climbing past $73,000. Investors are clearly using these peace signals as a reason to jump back in.

Big Money Funds XRP's Breakout

While the peace news started the rally, big money is keeping it going. On Tuesday, XRP spot ETFs saw $7.53 million in new cash.

Bitwise led the pack, bringing in over $6 million of that total. This shows that professional investors are feeling much better about XRP's future. At the same time, a new deal between Doppler Finance and Hex Trust is making it easier for big companies to use XRP on different blockchains. This gives the token more real-world utility, which helps the price stay strong.

XRP Flashes Technical Warnings amid the Rally

Despite all these positive signals and the excitement around peace talks, the data suggests caution. We took a look at XRP's technical indicators on TipRanks, and the picture is quite different from the headlines.

The technical summary currently shows a Strong Sell signal. Looking deeper into the numbers:

  • Oscillators are sitting in the Sell zone.
  • Moving Averages are flashing a Strong Sell, with 12 bearish indicators and zero bullish ones.
  • Overall, there are 15 Bearish signals compared to only 1 Bullish signal.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Mizuho Cuts Rigetti's Price Target after Q4 Sales Miss, Cites 'Superconducting Roadmap'
data.description:
Mizuho Securities' top analyst Vijay Rakesh lowered his price target on Rigetti Computing (RGTI) from $50 to $43 after the quantum computing firm missed sales expectations in its fiscal fourth quarter. He maintained his Buy rating stating that Rigetti remains on track with its superconducting roadmap. Even after the 14% cut, the target implies a strong 142% upside from current levels. Rakesh's revised $43 target equates to about 9x his projected sales 30 months out, assuming Rigetti captures 10% of the quantum computing market.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In Q4FY25, Rigetti posted a loss of $0.03 per share, in line with consensus estimates. However, revenue fell 17.6% year-over-year to $1.87 million, missing Wall Street's $2.33 million forecast.

Rakesh is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #28 out of 12,143 analysts tracked. He boasts a 64% success rate and an average return per rating of 47.50%.

Rakesh Optimistic on Long-Term Quantum Ramps

Rakesh views Rigetti as a fast-growing leader in superconducting quantum tech. He highlights its advantages in next-gen research funding and efficient chip designs. The company is on pace to deliver over 150 physical qubits by December 2026 and more than 1,000 by the end of 2027.

He acknowledged the weak December quarter results, but forecasts Q1 sales of $3 million, a 62% sequential increase and 106% year-over-year gain. This growth stems from Rigetti's $5.7 million Novera quantum processor contract. The firm will also deliver its first Cepheus-1 108-qubit system to India's Centre for Development of Advanced Computing for $8.4 million in the second half of 2026.

Rigetti recently achieved 99.9% fidelity on a quantum prototype using a new adiabatic CZ method at 28 nanoseconds, which is potentially 3 to 5 times faster than competitors. Key partnerships include Riverlane for improved error correction, and Nvidia (NVDA) for integrating quantum processors with GPUs/CPUs via NVLink, plus CUDA-Q software for hybrid systems.

Is RGTI Good Stock to Buy?

Analysts remain divided about Rigetti's outlook. On TipRanks, RGTI stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on five Buys and two Hold ratings. The average Rigetti Computing price target of $37.60 implies 111.7% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, RGTI shares have surged over 117%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: XRP Price Gains Ammunition as Middle East Conflict Sparks Demand for Fast Money
data.description:
The world is watching the Middle East with concern, but the crypto market is reacting in a very interesting way. While many stocks are shaky due to rising oil prices and war fears, XRP (XRP-USD) is showing its strength. Instead of just being a risky asset, XRP is getting new energy from its main purpose: moving money across borders instantly. When traditional banks get slow or nervous during a crisis, XRP's technology becomes more valuable than ever.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

XRP Provides Fast Money to Investors and Businesses

The main reason XRP gets ammunition from this conflict is its speed. In times of war, traditional banking systems can become clogged or restricted. Investors and businesses often need to move their wealth to safer places or different currencies right now.

The data shows that while Bitcoin is often seen as digital gold, XRP is viewed as the digital bridge. Because XRP can settle a transaction in just seconds for almost no cost, it acts as a tool for people who need to move liquidity fast when the world feels unpredictable. This real-world use keeps the demand for XRP high, even when other parts of the economy are falling.

Institutional Buying Hits Record Levels

Even as the conflict escalates, big financial players aren't backing away. In fact, many are doubling down on XRP.

New reports indicate that XRP ETFs have seen a record streak of money flowing in, with over $150 million added this year alone. Professional investors are beginning to see XRP as a core part of their portfolio because it is one of the few cryptocurrencies with clear legal rules and a working partnership with global banks. As long as there is a need for a fast way to move money between countries, XRP has a clear path to grow.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.4290.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market Futures Steady amid Middle East Uncertainty
data.description:
U.S. stock futures held steady Wednesday night as investors watched new military developments in the Middle East amid the expanding conflict involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 Index (SPX) were down 0.33%, 0.40%, and 0.30%, respectively, at 11:03 p.m. EST on March 4.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Earlier in the day, Wall Street had already posted solid gains. The rebound was driven by strength in big tech and semiconductor stocks. The Dow, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq rose 0.5%, 0.8%, and 1.3%, respectively.

Leading the gains were semiconductor stocks. Nvidia (NVDA) shares rose more than 1%. Other chipmakers, including Broadcom (AVGO), Micron Technology (MU), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Intel (INTC), also posted gains. Meanwhile, consumer staples, energy, and materials were the only S&P 500 sectors that ended the day in negative territory.

Investors now turn their attention to several major earnings reports due tomorrow. Kroger (KR), Burlington (BURL), and BJ's Wholesale (BJ) report Thursday morning, while Costco (COST) and Marvell Technology (MRVL) release results after the close. Weekly jobless claims are also due Thursday, adding another data point to a busy market week.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: AMD Stock Jumps on Meta Partnership & Strong AI Chip Sales
data.description:
AMD (AMD) is back on the rise after a busy day of big announcements. On March 5, the company's stock price jumped 5.7% during afternoon trading. This rally comes after a series of wins for its artificial intelligence (AI) business, including a huge partnership and a new line of processors. Despite some recent losses earlier in the week due to global news, investors are now focusing on AMD's growing role in the AI world.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meta Partnership and New AI Chips Drive Interest

The biggest news behind the jump is a massive deal between AMD and Meta Platforms (META). The two companies are teaming up for a 6-gigawatt project that uses AMD's Instinct GPUs to build out Meta's AI systems. CEO Lisa Su called the deal "transformational" for the company.

Beyond the Meta deal, AMD also released its new Ryzen AI 400 series processors. These chips are made specifically for the next generation of AI-powered laptops and computers. Lisa Su also noted that the demand for the company's processors has "far exceeded" what she expected, mostly because so many businesses are rushing to buy AI tech.

AMD Expands with New Research and Investments

AMD is also putting more money into the future of AI. The company just announced a $150 million investment into a partnership with Nutanix (NTNX) to build a new AI platform. Additionally, it is launching a new research lab at the University of Toronto to stay ahead in the chip-making race.

The market data shows that the stock closed the day at $202.14. While the stock is still down about 9.5% for the year, this recent move shows that the market is excited about AMD's AI progress. This gain helps recover some of the losses from earlier in the week, when oil price spikes and war fears caused a broad sell-off across the Nasdaq (NDAQ).

Is AMD Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to TipRanks, the analysts' consensus rating for AMD is Moderate Buy, based on 23 Buy and eight Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average AMD stock price target of $286.04, representing a potential 41.55% upside for the shares.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Cathie Wood Dumps $40M of ROKU, Pumps $14M in Amazon
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio adjustments on Wednesday, March 4, as shown in ARK's daily fund disclosures. Wood executed high-volume buy and sell trades across global stocks worth millions, capitalizing on price dips fueled by escalating Iran tensions. Here's a quick overview of her trades.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's What Wood Sold on Wednesday

The largest trade of the day was the sale of 412,711 shares of TV-streaming platform Roku Inc. (ROKU) for about $40 million, locking in gains after its 3.7% surge yesterday. Its shares have been highly volatile since reporting fiscal fourth-quarter results on February 14. Wood has been actively reducing exposure to Roku over the past couple of weeks.

She also offloaded 13,663 shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) for roughly $4.89 million, while its stock price was up 1.2%. Shares had reversed course after the prior day's 5.5% dip due to geopolitical tensions.

Wood continued to reduce the fund's stake in Iridium Communications (IRDM), selling 202,146 shares worth about $4.95 million.

What Did Wood Buy on March 4?

On the buy side, ARK's largest trade was the purchase of 66,934 shares of American e-commerce and cloud-computing giant Amazon (AMZN) for $14.51 million. Amazon's data centers were hit by drone attacks in Dubai amid the Middle East war. However, AMZN stock gained 3.9% after announcing an additional $21 billion investment in Spain.

ARK also bought 176,884 shares of gaming platform Roblox (RBLX) for about $11.92 million. It acquired 542,828 shares of Genius Sports (GENI) for $3.28 million, capitalizing on the sports data and technology company's 6.7% drop. These moves align with Wood's growing conviction in the gaming and technology sectors.

Other smaller trades included buying 200,856 shares of Joby Aviation (JOBY) for nearly $2 million and 419,919 shares of Archer Aviation (ACHR) totaling $2.84 million.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool.

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on Genius Sports, Amazon, TSM, and ROKU. Among them, GENI stock has the highest upside potential over the next twelve months, but it is a loss-making company. AMZN and ROKU stocks also offer more than 30% upside potential.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: AMD Stock Slides as New Nvidia GPU Orders Grab Attention
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) edged lower in after-hours trading Wednesday after the stock had surged about 6% during the regular session as semiconductor shares rebounded alongside the broader technology sector.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The earlier rally came as oil prices eased, helping lift investor sentiment after geopolitical tensions earlier in the week had pressured growth stocks. As energy prices stabilized, technology shares recovered, pushing the Nasdaq Composite higher and supporting chipmakers such as AMD.

However, AMD slipped in extended trading as investors reacted to fresh developments in the AI chip market.

Nvidia Orders May Have Weighed on AMD

One key announcement came from Iris Energy (IREN), which said it has signed purchase agreements for more than 50,000 GPUs from Nvidia (NVDA) to expand its AI cloud infrastructure.

Large orders like this can influence sentiment in the AI chip space because AMD competes directly with Nvidia in data-center accelerators, the chips used to train and run artificial-intelligence models.

Nvidia currently dominates that market, which has made investor reactions to major GPU orders particularly sensitive.

For investors, the key question now is whether AMD can continue gaining traction in the fast-growing AI accelerator market, where it is trying to challenge Nvidia's lead.

Is AMD Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for AMD is Moderate Buy, based on 23 Buy and eight Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average AMD stock price target of $286.04, representing a potential 41.55% upside for the shares.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Robinhood Stock (HOOD) Rises as $695 'Actual' Platinum Card Challenges Amex and JPM
data.description:
Robinhood Markets (HOOD) is entering the premium credit card market with its new $695 Platinum Card, aiming to challenge heavyweights like American Express (AXP) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM). Ahead of the launch, HOOD stock jumped 8% on Wednesday, fueled by anticipation of the event and a strong Bitcoin rally, and added another 2% in after-hours trading following the official announcement.

data.title: MU, SNDK Stocks Gain after Intel CFO New Remarks — What Investors Should Know
data.description:
Memory chip stocks Micron Technology (MU) and SanDisk (SNDK) gained about 6% each on Wednesday after new comments from Intel (INTC) signaled that the industry may be facing a prolonged shortage of memory chips.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference, Intel CFO David Zinsner said the semiconductor industry is seeing memory shortages "all over the place." He added that supply constraints could persist through 2027. Zinsner noted that the shortage could hit PCs the hardest, as demand for memory components continues to rise while supply remains constrained.

For memory manufacturers such as Micron and SanDisk, tight supply often leads to higher pricing and stronger profitability, which helps explain why the stocks moved higher following the comments.

Micron's Upcoming Earnings Report Will Offer More Clarity

Memory chips are essential for a wide range of products, including data-center servers, AI systems, and personal computers. As demand for these technologies grows, investors are closely watching signs that the memory market could tighten further.

It is also worth noting that Micron is set to report its Q2 FY26 earnings on March 18. Wall Street analysts expect earnings of about $8.54 per share, up roughly 447% from the year-ago quarter, while revenue is projected to reach around $18.88 billion, representing 135% year-over-year growth.

Micron's results and management's commentary could provide investors with greater clarity on memory demand, pricing trends, and the broader supply-demand balance in the chip market.

For now, Intel's comments suggest that memory supply shortages could persist across the semiconductor industry for the next few years.

Which Is the Better Memory Stock, According to Analysts?

By using TipRanks' Comparison Tool, we've compared the two memory-chip stocks discussed above. Micron currently carries a Strong Buy analyst consensus with about 6.4% upside, while SanDisk has a Moderate Buy rating with roughly 6.3% upside.

Investors can dig deeper to decide which memory stock best fits their strategy. Below is a screenshot for reference.

data.title: Anthropic and Pentagon Talk Again after Big Fight Over AI Safety
data.description:
The big argument between the Pentagon and Anthropic might finally be over. Today, news came out that the two sides are back at the table. Just last week, the government called the startup a supply chain risk and compared them to an enemy country. Now, they are trying to find a way to work together that lets the military use the tech without breaking the company's safety rules.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Anthropic Demands Strict Limits on AI Use

The trouble started because Anthropic insisted on setting firm boundaries. The company wanted a promise that the military would not use its AI to spy on Americans or to run weapons that shoot without a person in control.

Dario Amodei, the head of Anthropic, was talking with defense leaders, but those talks stopped when he wouldn't budge on these safety rules. Because of that, the government blocked the company. This move upset many other tech giants like Google (GOOGL) and Apple (AAPL). They told the government that punishing a company for trying to be safe is a bad idea for everyone.

Anthropic Hits Record Growth Despite Drama

While Anthropic was fighting with the government, its rival OpenAI signed a deal to put its own AI into the military's secret networks.

The data shows that even with all this fighting, Anthropic is still making a lot of money. The company is now worth $380 billion and is expected to bring in $20 billion this year. Its phone app is also at the top of the charts, which means regular people still trust the company even if the government is being tough.

The company is also hiring more workers and has seen its online following grow, according to new numbers on TipRanks that show "positive" momentum for the business.
data.author: Annika Masrani
